title: Email Automation: Changing Sender Email to a Group Email
excerpt: I’m looking to set up an automation to send an email once a form is submitted. This is a form that will be used by several people in our organization, so I’d like for it to not be sent from my email account. Is there a w…

posts.author: avanoo (Alina V)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m looking to set up an automation to send an email once a form is submitted. This is a form that will be used by several people in our organization, so I’d like for it to not be sent from my email account. Is there a way to change the senders email when I want to use a group email instead? I know I can use a different account, but the email that would make the most sense is a group in Outlook. Example arts@compay.org goes to three different people, but doesn’t actually have log in information.

Thanks for the help!

posts.author: noam.lapidot (Noam Lapidot)
posts.cookedHtml:
@greentoothbrush @avanoo
In order to send automated emails from a group email address you can use the Supermail add on. It’s a native monday.com app that you can install from the marketplace and supports this functionality.

auth.monday.com

SuperMail - monday.com Marketplace

Emails: Design, Send and Track directly from monday.com

It will also allow u sending designed emails, attachments, hyperlinks and even embed buttons in email body, that WI trigger a status change.
